meaning: vt.激怒,挑拨,煽动,惹起,驱使
examples: His duty was to conciliate the people, not to provoke them.
Premature moves in this respect might well provoke a reaction against the reform.
They accused both radicals and conservatives of trying to provoke a split in the party.
"If you question someone for eight hours, you can provoke him into anything, " he said.
CD-quality sound on the company's instant-messaging service could provoke even steamier gossip around the coffee pot.
